topic: Git History Analysis in Developer Tools

sources.title: Code as a Crime Scene - Adam Tornhill
keyInsights: Geographical profiling from criminal psychology applied to code analysis
Version control as behavioral record, not just versioning
Logical coupling reveals hidden dependencies invisible to static analysis
Social analysis: developer count, knowledge drain, parallel activity as quality signals
Complexity only matters when combined with change frequency
Tree maps and Code City metaphor for visualization

sources.title: CodeScene Technical Debt and Hotspot Analysis Documentation
keyInsights: Hotspots combine development frequency with code health metrics
1.2% of codebase can consume 12.5% of development effort and 45% of bugs
Code health weighted by temporal activity data
Integration with Jira for defect correlation and cost assignment
Team-level filtering and organizational data integration

sources.title: CodeScene Change Coupling Documentation
keyInsights: Three coupling detection mechanisms: commit-level, temporal, ticket-based
Sum of couplings as primary metric for architectural centrality
Color-coded trends: red (strengthening), blue (weakening), yellow (stable)
Use cases: clone detection, test relevance, architectural decay, hidden dependencies
Cross-team coordination bottleneck detection

sources.title: Hercules - Git Repository Analysis Engine
keyInsights: DAG-based analysis pipeline over full commit history including branches and merges
Burndown analysis at repo, file, and developer levels
File and developer coupling matrices
Sentiment analysis of code comments via neural networks
Dynamic Time Warping for developer similarity, HDBSCAN clustering
Linux kernel analysis in ~100 minutes
Output in YAML, Protocol Buffers, JSON, TSV

sources.title: git-of-theseus - Analyze How a Git Repo Grows Over Time
keyInsights: Code survival tracking via Kaplan-Meier estimation with exponential decay fitting
Cohort analysis grouping code by year written
Author contribution persistence tracking
Output as JSON files: cohorts.json, authors.json, exts.json, survival.json
Stack plots and line plots for visualization

sources.title: 500x Faster Theseus Plots with Rust and Gitoxide
keyInsights: 500-850x speedup over Python git-of-theseus using Rust and gitoxide
Incremental blame tracking instead of repeated git blame calls
Linux repo: 36000s -> 68s; FFmpeg: 8195s -> 9.6s
Single-pass history traversal maintaining blame state
Focused scope reduction for performance

sources.title: GitClear - Measuring Code Activity: A Comprehensive Guide
keyInsights: Diff Delta correlates 26-61% with developer effort vs 13-31% for lines of code
Values removed code equally with added code
Code churn identified as problematic metric that conflates multiple concepts
65+ velocity, AI usage, code quality, and DevEx metrics
2172 developer-weeks of AI productivity correlation analysis

sources.title: Focus Refactoring on What Matters with Hotspots Analysis
keyInsights: Churn vs Complexity quadrant model with four actionable zones
Practical git command for computing file churn from last 12 months
Top-right quadrant (high churn + high complexity) is the refactoring priority
Complex but stable code doesn't cost money - focus on active hotspots
Data-driven approach prevents 'everything is urgent' paralysis

sources.title: commit-prophet: Predicting Buggy Files Using Git History
keyInsights: Three weighted signals: churn (40%), defect coupling (50%), co-change (10%)
Bug-fix detection via commit message keywords (fix, bug, patch, crash)
File pair co-occurrence computed via itertools.combinations
0-100 risk scores with labels: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
90-day analysis windows for co-change detection
Pure Python with subprocess calls to git

sources.title: code-forensics Coupling Analysis Documentation
keyInsights: Coupling degree derived from shared commits within a period
Two analysis types: sum of coupling (global) and temporal coupling (targeted)
Interactive enclosure diagrams with color intensity for coupling strength
Combined visualization of coupling degree with code churn metrics
Time-split analysis showing coupling evolution across periods

sources.title: truckfactor - Compute the Truck Factor of a Git Repository
keyInsights: Knowledge ownership = developer who edited most lines in each file
Iterative removal algorithm with >50% file coverage threshold
Output: truck factor number, critical authors list, commit SHA
CSV and verbose output formats
Based on Avelino et al. 'A novel approach for estimating Truck Factors'

sources.title: How Can You Measure Conway's Law? - CodeScene Blog
keyInsights: Team coupling measured by overlapping code contributions to same components
Color-coded visualization: redder = more team coupling
Strong coupling indicates design issues or org-architecture misalignment
Aggregation from individual authors to team-level boundaries
Data-driven decisions about team formation and service splitting

sources.title: Git Analytics: Challenges, Tools & Key Metrics
keyInsights: DORA metrics, SPACE framework, and flow metrics as foundations
Activity != productivity - high activity could mean unnecessary bloat
Track teams not individuals to prevent toxic metrics culture
Balance quantitative git data with qualitative developer feedback
Investment distribution tracking across features, refactoring, maintenance

sources.title: Conventional Commits Specification v1.0.0
keyInsights: Structured format: type[scope]: description [body] [footer]
Core types: fix (PATCH), feat (MINOR), BREAKING CHANGE (MAJOR)
Machine-parseable for automated changelog and version bump
Compatible with semantic versioning
Adopted by Angular, commitlint, semantic-release ecosystem

sources.title: PyDriller - Python Framework for Mining Software Repositories
keyInsights: Extract commits, developers, modifications, diffs, source code
Filter by date range, commit hash, tag, author
Structural metrics via Lizard integration
Traverse full history or filtered subsets
Academic origin with ACM ESEC/FSE 2018 publication

sources.title: GitEvo: Code Evolution Analysis for Git Repositories
keyInsights: Four-step pipeline: commit selection, parsing, metric computation, export
Combines git-level (GitPython, PyDriller) with code-level (tree-sitter) analysis
Tracks evolution of functions, classes, decorators, type annotations
Supports Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java
Custom metric API for extensibility

sources.title: RepoSense - Contribution Analysis Tool for Git Repositories
keyInsights: Chronological visualization of code contributions and commits
Static HTML report generation
Contribution frequency and amount visualization
Code review panel and author comparison
Originated at NUS School of Computing for educational use

sources.title: gitinspector - Statistical Analysis Tool for Git Repositories
keyInsights: Per-author statistics with timeline analysis
Workload and activity visualization per author
Multi-threaded analysis using multiple git instances
Output in plain text, HTML, and XML
Originally for university grading, now used worldwide

sources.title: JITBot: An Explainable Just-In-Time Defect Prediction Bot (ASE 2020)
keyInsights: Automatic commit risk scoring with explanations
Risk mitigation plan suggestions per commit
Integration into GitHub CI/CD as a GitHub App
Just-In-Time prediction at commit time, not retrospective
Continuous monitoring across multiple repositories

sources.title: Git History Analyzer and Code Quality Predictor - GitHub Action
keyInsights: ML-powered commit pattern and code churn analysis
Configurable risk thresholds (high: 0.8, medium: 0.5)
Automatic PR comments with analysis results
Identifies bug hotspots, refactoring candidates, test coverage gaps
Triggered on push/pull request events

sources.title: Bus Factor of Top GitHub Projects - Metabase Blog
keyInsights: 65% of GitHub projects have bus factor of 2 or less
Almost half have bus factor of 2 or less
Analysis across top GitHub projects
Quantifies knowledge concentration risk at ecosystem level

sources.title: truegitcodechurn - Compute True Code Churn of a Git Repository
keyInsights: Distinguishes 'true' churn (rework of recently-written code) from normal changes
Python script for churn computation
Separates productive changes from wasteful rework
Configurable time window for 'recent' definition

sources.title: Your Code as a Crime Scene, Second Edition - Pragmatic Bookshelf
keyInsights: Forensic techniques applied to code: geographical profiling, behavioral analysis
Predict future of codebase from version control patterns
Assess refactoring direction with data
Understand how team dynamics influence code design
Language-agnostic analysis techniques

sources.title: code-forensics - Code Analysis and Report Visualization Toolset
keyInsights: Node.js-based analysis running on gulp 4
Language-agnostic for most analyses
Sloc-trend, complexity-trend, developer-effort analysis tasks
Web-based report visualization
Time interval configuration for analysis periods
